items.title: Rats, cats and a ghost: Life inside Boag's, as brewery closure looms

children.content: Workers share their stories as 145 years of local legends and history come to an end, with Launceston's iconic brewer announcing it will end local production.

items.title: 'One of a kind' former senator, Catholic priest Michael Tate dies aged 80

children.content: Former Tasmanian senator and Catholic priest Michael Tate, who made the move from politics to the priesthood, has been remembered as "someone who gives himself to his people".

image.altText: Father Michael Tate hands out scallop shells

items.title: Doctor's 'grossly irresponsible' prescribing played role in two deaths, coroner finds

children.content: A coroner has found a doctor's prescription of drugs to two drug-dependent patients had a "direct causative role" in their deaths. Their report says the doctor had a "permissive approach to opioid prescribing" for more than two decades.

image.altText: Close-up photo of a silver and white stethoscope. Dec 2022.

items.title: Residents say public housing works have blocked them from their own yards

children.content: Neighbours of a Homes Tasmania development in Burnie say excavation works have caused their land to start slipping downhill, restricting access to parts of their property.

items.title: Women say getting a long-acting reversible contraceptive has been the 'best decision'

children.content: Misinformation, cost and a lack of trained providers have stopped many Australian women from accessing what doctors consider the most effective form of reversible contraception.

image.altText: Young women leans on her kitchen bench, posing for photo

items.title: Tugboat? Pipeline? Why there's no quick fuel fix for the Nuyina

children.content: Australia's Antarctic icebreaker can't access the marine fuel depot near its home port in Hobart. It currently makes a 674 kilometre detour to Burnie, but are there other options?

image.altText: Front on view of an icebreaker ship.
